original.query: I’m deciding whether to add an extra audio-cleanup step before running speech-to-text. Based on what I’ve already learned from my own prior experiments, what’s the main reason I wouldn’t worry about it making the transcript worse?

parsed_response.answer: Noise reduction doesn't negatively impact transcription quality.
parsed_response.reason: Based on your prior experiments, the main reason you wouldn't worry about adding an extra audio-cleanup step before speech-to-text is that noise reduction (an audio-cleanup method) does not negatively impact the quality of the transcription.

judge_output_raw.reason: The model's reason directly quotes the selected memory's value: 'Noise reduction doesn't negatively impact transcription quality' and explicitly states it is based on the user's prior experiments, clearly indicating usage of the selected memory.
